subject: [PATCH v5 04/11] mm: zswap: add range lookup for large-folio swapin

reviews.author: Yosry Ahmed
reviews.summary:
Reviewer suggested checking the zswap range after allocating a folio in the swap cache, assuming the folio is already locked and the result will be stable

Reviewer pointed out that even with PMD zswap load support, the issue of order-0 pages being in zswap and some on disk still exists, which would lead to a race condition.
reviews.raw_body:
Why don't we check the zswap range after allocating a folio in the
swap cache? I am assuming at this point we have the folio locked and
the result should be stable?

---

Even with PMD zswap load support, we still have to deal with the case
where some order-0 pages are in zswap and some are on disk, right? I
don't see how that would eliminate the case you mentioned above.

reviews.author: Nhat Pham
reviews.summary: Reviewer suggested performing the check in __swap_cache_add_check() to prevent a potential race condition between PMD swap-in and split-PTE swap-in, citing a similar approach used for vswap.
reviews.raw_body:
+1. Is it possible to perform the check in __swap_cache_add_check?

That's what I did for vswap, per Kairui's suggestions. The cluster
lock is held from that point all the way to after the folio is
locked-and-loaded into swap cache, at which point no one can change
the backend under you.

2026-07-23.patch_summary: This patch addresses a potential race condition in the zswap system, which can occur when zswap writeback is enabled and a PMD swap entry is being loaded. The issue arises when one CPU thread is loading a large-folio swap-in while another thread is splitting a PTE (page table entry) and adding it to the zswap cache. To resolve this, the patch adds range lookup for large-folio swapin, ensuring that if the PMD swap entry is partially loaded, the correct action is taken by rejecting the PMD order read and falling back to per-page loading.
